attributes.readMe:
## Stepper

A general multi-step flow shell — the "guideline book" that walks a user through a journey (a first-run wizard, an onboarding flow, a setup checklist) *before* they start using an app. Two-pane layout: a vertical step rail on the left (numbered dots, ✓ done state, active ring, connector lines) and the active step's content on the right, closed out by a Back / Skip / Next action bar built from boxel-ui `<Button>`.

The first consumer is the Table Seating Planner's setup wizard; the component carries no wedding-specific anything — every product supplies its own steps, content, and brand.

### Ownership split

The **host** owns the step definitions (labels, completion state) and each step's content, supplied through the `<:step>` named block. The **Stepper** owns the step state machine, the rail's done/active painting, gating Next on `isComplete`, and the shell chrome (header, action bar, optional modal scrim). The component never closes itself — visibility stays with the host, exactly like Popover's `@open`.

### Steps — `StepperStep[]`

```ts
interface StepperStep {
  id: string;            // dispatch key for the <:step> block
  label: string;         // rail label ('Your event')
  summary?: string;      // rail sub-line while pending; swaps to 'Completed' when done
  title?: string;        // content-pane heading (defaults to label)
  description?: string;  // content-pane sub-line
  icon?: ComponentLike;  // lead icon circle above the title
  isComplete?: boolean;  // gates Next + paints the rail ✓; omit = never gates
  optional?: boolean;    // shows a Skip button on this step (MUI-style)
}
```

- **Completion gating is host-wired**: point `isComplete` at your own state (`this.name.length > 0`, `this.guests.length > 0`) and Next unlocks/locks live.
- **Skip is per-step opt-in** (`optional: true`), following the MUI Stepper convention — linear steps get no Skip button. `api.skip` remains available for custom action bars.

### Args

| Arg | Purpose |
| --- | --- |
| `@steps` | the `StepperStep[]` above |
| `@modal` | wrap in a scrim + centered card (`position: absolute; inset: 0` — fills the nearest positioned ancestor). Default inline bordered card |
| `@kicker` / `@title` | header eyebrow + brand name; header renders only when one of these (or the close button) is present |
| `@onClose` / `@showClose` | ✕ close — an outlined 30px circle, no fill (same treatment as the seating planner's popover close). `@showClose` defaults to true whenever `@onClose` is set |
| `@finishLabel` / `@onFinish` | the last step swaps Skip/Next for a single primary button labeled `finishLabel` (default 'Done') |
| `@onStepChange` | fires `(step, index)` on every step entry — use for enter hooks (e.g. lazy-load data when the user reaches a step) |
| `@initialStep` | start on a later step (resume) |
| `@allowStepJump` | click rail items to jump between steps. Off by default so wizards stay linear |

### Blocks

- `<:step as |step api|>` — the active step's content; dispatch on `step.id`. The `api` bundle exposes `next` / `back` / `skip` / `goTo` plus `index` / `isFirst` / `isLast` / `canProceed` for hosts that want custom controls inside the content.
- `<:actions as |api|>` — replaces the default action bar entirely.
- `<:decoration>` — renders inside the card for brand art (the seating planner puts its gold corner mandala here).

### Accent & contrast

Fills (done dots, connectors, the lead icon circle) paint with the accent; the content ON those fills (✓ glyph, icon) uses a separate foreground so it always reads:

- The fills follow the linked theme's `--primary` with `--primary-foreground` as the on-fill content — themes author this pair as a contrast pairing. With no theme, the fallback is `--boxel-highlight` (bright green) with a dark on-fill color — correct WCAG pairing.
- For a one-off brand override, set the `--stepper-accent` / `--stepper-accent-fg` pair on a host class (the seating planner sets gold + white).
- Text on the light rail (active label, kicker) stays ink/muted — accent is for fills only, never light-background text.

### Theming — the theme system drives it

Every visual value resolves through three scopes, strongest first:

1. **Stepper-only knobs** — `--stepper-*` custom properties set on a host class restyle ONLY the stepper: `--stepper-accent` · `--stepper-accent-fg` · `--stepper-ink` · `--stepper-muted` · `--stepper-surface` (rail bg) · `--stepper-card-bg` · `--stepper-border` · `--stepper-connector` · `--stepper-primary-bg` / `--stepper-primary-fg` (Next button) · `--stepper-kicker-color` · `--stepper-heading-font` · `--stepper-body-font` · `--stepper-radius` · `--stepper-scrim-bg` · `--stepper-shadow`
2. **Theme semantic tokens** — link a Theme card / Brand Guide and the stepper follows automatically: `--primary` / `--primary-foreground` drive the accent fills, their on-fill content, and the Next button; `--foreground`, `--muted`, `--muted-foreground`, `--card`, `--border`, `--radius`, and `--font-sans` drive text, surfaces, hairlines, corners, and type.
3. **Built-in neutral defaults** (Boxel tokens) — used with no theme at all.

The seating planner's Parisian look (navy/gold, Cormorant + Jost) is a scope-1 override — see `wz-theme` in its setup-wizard for the full block.

## Usage

The **Stepper Playground** example is the canonical reference — knobs for `@modal` and `@allowStepJump` over a live 4-step demo whose gating is real, with a generated-invocation panel; its colors follow the linked theme. The minimal shape:

```gts
import Stepper from '../stepper';
import type { StepperStep } from '../stepper';

get steps(): StepperStep[] {
  return [
    { id: 'event', label: 'Your event', summary: 'Event details',
      description: 'Tell us about it.', icon: CalendarIcon,
      isComplete: !!this.title, optional: true },
    { id: 'template', label: 'Template', summary: 'Choose a layout' },
  ];
}

<template>
  <Stepper
    @modal={{true}}
    @steps={{this.steps}}
    @kicker='Getting started'
    @title='My Product'
    @finishLabel='Start blank'
    @onClose={{this.dismiss}}
    @onFinish={{this.dismiss}}
    @onStepChange={{this.stepEntered}}
  >
    <:step as |step|>
      {{#if (eq step.id 'event')}} …event form… {{else}} …template list… {{/if}}
    </:step>
  </Stepper>
</template>
```

Cross-realm import: `@cardstack/catalog/aef6db-stepper/stepper`.
attributes.cardDescription: A multi-step flow shell for first-run wizards and onboarding journeys: vertical step rail, per-step completion gating, MUI-style optional steps, boxel-ui buttons, and theme-driven styling (--primary/--primary-foreground pair) with --stepper-* override knobs.
